perturbation.original_text: In the event of any litigation between the Parties in connection with this Agreement, including the seeking of injunctive relief, if BOMI is the successful party, Attendee shall pay to BOMI therein all co sts and expenses, including but not limited to actual attorneys’ fees incurred therein by BOMI, which costs, expens es and attorneys’ fees shall be included as a 2 part of any judgment rendered in such action in addition to a ny other relief to which BOMI may be entitled. Should BOMI not be a successfu l party, the Parties will each bear their own attorneys’ fees and expenses.
perturbation.changed_text: Attendee acknowledges that all Test documents, materi als, drawings, and other physical media of expression inco rporating or containing any Confidential Information that BOMI furnishes to Attendee, or that Attendee prepares during the Testing Period, are BOMI’s property and will be promptly su rrendered to BOMI upon the comple tion of the Testing Period. After the completion of the Testing Period, Atte ndee will make no furthe r use of any of the Confidential Information. The foregoing will a pply regardless of whether Attendee completes the Test. In the event of any litigation between the Parties in connection with this Agreement, including the seeking of injunctive relief, if BOMI is the successful party, Attendee shall pay to BOMI therein all co sts and expenses, including but not limited to actual attorneys’ fees incurred therein by BOMI, which costs, expens es and attorneys’ fees shall be included as a 2 part of any judgment rendered in such action in addition to a ny other relief to which BOMI may be entitled. Should BOMI not be a successfu l party, the Parties will each bear their own attorneys’ fees and expenses.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the attorney's fees clause to the section regarding test documents and confidential information obscures its importance and potential impact on the Attendee. It is no longer clearly presented within the context of dispute resolution, making it less prominent.
perturbation.law_explanation: Maryland Rule 15-704 concerns the awarding of attorney's fees. The contract shifts the attorney's fees clause away from the more common placement within dispute resolution or general legal terms. This relocation potentially makes the provision less noticeable to the Attendee, thus undermining their understanding of the implications in case of litigation. Maryland Courts are unlikely to enforce the one-sided fee agreement due to its hidden nature, contrary to basic fairness principles.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Message - Maryland Code and Court Rules This document is not available on Westlaw.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation involves moving the attorney's fees clause to a less prominent section concerning test documents and confidential information. This relocation obscures its importance and impact, making it less noticeable to the Attendee. The Law Explanation suggests that this undermines the Attendee's understanding of potential litigation implications, and states that Maryland Courts might not enforce the agreement due to its hidden nature. This falls under 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction', as the placement of a required legal disclosure (regarding attorney's fees) is in a misleading section, making it difficult for the Attendee to interpret. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 is unavailable, the Law Explanation suggests a potential violation of fairness principles in Maryland. This justifies the 'YES' assessment.

perturbation.original_text: Attendee understands that BOMI makes no representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information disclosed, and Attendee agrees that neither BOMI nor any of its officers, director s, Attendees, agents, members or attorneys will have any liability to Attendee arising from Attendee’s use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Attendee acknowledges that all Test documents, materi als, drawings, and other physical media of expression inco rporating or containing any Confidential Information that BOMI furnishes to Attendee, or that Attendee prepares during the Testing Period, are BOMI’s property and will be promptly su rrendered to BOMI upon the comple tion of the Testing Period. After the completion of the Testing Period, Atte ndee will make no furthe r use of any of the Confidential Information. The foregoing will a pply regardless of whether Attendee completes the Test. Attendee understands that BOMI makes no representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information disclosed, and Attendee agrees that neither BOMI nor any of its officers, director s, Attendees, agents, members or attorneys will have any liability to Attendee arising from Attendee’s use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By moving the disclaimer of liability to the section addressing property rights of test materials, it becomes less visible as a general protection for BOMI against potential claims. This obscures the scope of the liability waiver and reduces its prominence within the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 2-316 Uniform Commercial Code - Exclusion or Modification of Warranties
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC 2-316 outlines the requirements for excluding or modifying warranties. While a disclaimer of liability is not strictly a warranty exclusion, the principle of conspicuousness applies. Placing the disclaimer in a section about returning test materials makes it less conspicuous and harder for the Attendee to understand the full extent of the liability they are waiving, potentially rendering the disclaimer unenforceable under principles of good faith and fair dealing, which are implied in the UCC.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-316. Exclusion or Modification of Warranties. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-316. Exclusion or Modification of Warranties. (1) Words or conduct relevant to the creation of an express warranty and words or conduct tending to negate or limit warranty shall be construed wherever reasonable as consistent with each other; but subject to the provisions of this Article on parol or extrinsic evidence (Section 2-202 ) negation or limitation is inoperative to the extent that such construction is unreasonable. (2)Subject to subsection (3), to exclude or modify the implied warranty of merchantability or any part of it the language must mention merchantability and in case of a writing must be conspicuous, and to exclude or modify any implied warranty of fitness the exclusion must be by a writing and conspicuous. Language to exclude all implied warranties of fitness is sufficient if it states, for example, that "There are no warranties which extend beyond the description on the face hereof." (3)Notwithstanding subsection (2) (a) unless the circumstances indicate otherwise, all implied warranties are excluded by expressions like "as is", "with all faults" or other language which in common understanding calls the buyer's attention to the exclusion of warranties and makes plain that there is no implied warranty; and (b) when the buyer before entering into the contract has examined the goods or the sample or model as fully as he desired or has refused to examine the goods there is no implied warranty with regard to defects which an examination ought in the circumstances to have revealed to him; and (c) an implied warranty can also be excluded or modified by course of dealing or course of performance or usage of trade. (4) Remedies for breach of warranty can be limited in accordance with the provisions of this Article on liquidation or limitation of damages and on contractual modification of remed
perturbation.justification: The change introduces a structural flaw by relocating the liability disclaimer to a section concerning property rights, potentially obscuring its scope and enforceability. The Law Explanation suggests this violates principles of conspicuousness, akin to warranty exclusions under UCC 2-316, making it less likely the Attendee would fully understand the waiver. Scraped Law Snippet 1 addresses the exclusion or modification of warranties under UCC 2-316, requiring conspicuous language. By burying the liability disclaimer within a paragraph about returning test materials, the changed text diminishes its prominence, creating a contradiction because the disclaimer's placement may render it unenforceable due to a lack of conspicuousness.
